defs: The wood of the cork oak.
Any one of several trees or shrubs having light or corky wood;

In the United States, the tree Leitneria floridana, a very small deciduous dioecious tree or shrub of damp habitats in the southeastern US having extremely light wood; -- called also the corkwood tree.
In the West Indies: (1) Either of the cotton trees Ochroma lagopus and Pariti tiliaceum.
The tree producing the aligator apple.
